dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: You run into anything bad whilst you're out there, you come talk to me about it. Chances are I've seen it, fought it, otherwise gotten past it, and can offer some advice.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: You and the Goddess Artemis make quite a force out there by all accounts. And you sing together beautifully, at that...! Olympus sent her to check up on us, then, aye?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I think so, yes. But I have complete trust in her if that's what you're wondering. She tells Olympus no more than they need to know to rest assured about our partnership.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: She is by nature rather cautious, isn't she? Well for my part, I'm pleased you have in her not just an ally... but a friend.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: That assassin-Shade you ran into in the woods... a Master-Slicer from Ephyra, and one of the fiercest servants of Chronos. They're trying to stop you more aggressively.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: We'll see that wretch cast down to Tartarus before all this is over. Any other sightings in Erebus to be concerned about?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Nothing conclusive, though we're watching. Fortunately we have Nemesis to help keep an eye on things, don't we?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: If I may confide something, Goddess? Throughout my mortal life, I never wanted to depend on anyone. But, I always had aid... from great warriors, sailors, even gods! Yet... even still, it's difficult to ask assistance in personal matters.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I know the feeling well. A sense of weakness... the inability to do something oneself. We strive for self-sufficience and we don't wish to heap our troubles on our comrades.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Aye, we know it isn't weakness, but... we have to fight our instincts there a bit, don't we? Though mainly, what I wished to say was, thank you. For the aid you've given me. You needn't have gone out of your way.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: We've not too many Shades all the way out on the Olympus front, only a few brave souls. They're saying that you met Lady Athena face-to-face, how is she holding up?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Considering she's holding back great numbers of the Titan's forces single-handedly, she seems remarkably composed.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: I'd ask you thank her for the times she may have saved my skin, but... discretion's for the best.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: You're really using that labrys, Goddess? Or, pardon, what you call the Moonstone Axe. No disrespect, but isn't it... a little much? Not even Ajax could have wielded such a thing.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Weren't you the one who taught me to improvise, Odysseus? I'll use whatever means at my disposal. Who is Ajax?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Who's Ajax? Only the biggest lad I've ever met, who's Ajax. But I forget myself, for he didn't have the strength of Hades flowing through him.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: How has our map been working out for you thus far? You reached Oceanus, sure, though the path ahead grows still more labyrinthine based on all we've ascertained.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: There seem as many pathways in the Underworld as stars in the night sky... yet your map has shown the way precisely. You must have an excellent sense of direction.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh I don't know, but if our map helps overcompensate for my own lack, then good. Though, careful when you reach the Mourning Fields. No map shall benefit you there.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: We've ceased attempts to find a good route through Elysium, hence your somewhat unconventional way into Tartarus. The old administrative corridor remains neglected still?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Completely deserted, to the point of being rather peaceful. Drops me right into the maintenance sector, just as you mapped out.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, our Shades deserve the greatest share of credit for that feat, though I'll take some if you insist. Your task is difficult enough that we didn't need you getting lost along the way.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Getting reports that a certain oversized and very angry deathless boar found its way into Ephyra, which should not be possible when the entire city's shut. You've seen it, aye?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: It's definitely the Boar of Erymanthus. Somehow it didn't seem too out-of-place within a city of the dead. Better it be stuck there for now than rampaging across the land.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: It ought to be beneath the earth where it might finally find peace, but for now, it's very restless and liable to stay that way. Do mind the tusks and hooves.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Getting reports that a certain oversized and very angry deathless boar found its way into Ephyra, which should not be possible when the entire city's shut. You've seen it, aye?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: It's definitely the Boar of Erymanthus. Somehow it didn't seem too out-of-place within a city of the dead. Better it be stuck there for now than rampaging across the land.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: It ought to be beneath the earth where it might finally find peace, but for now, it's very restless and liable to stay that way. Do mind the tusks and hooves.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: If I may be so bold, Goddess? You witches are reliant on your magick when it comes to fighting from afar. Ever think of taking up the bow again? I could give you some pointers...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: We don't discuss the bow, Odysseus. I'm still recovering from the last time you gave me some of those pointers about it.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Ah, well. We all have our strengths and weaknesses. A single weakness in your case, perhaps.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: A word of caution, Goddess, in regard to an impediment you may confront when finally you reach the far end of the Mourning Fields. Reports suggest it's some sort of infernal beast... a big one. Our Shades had to stay very far away.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: We knew there was a secure entrance into Tartarus around that point... this beast must be how come the tunnel stays secure. Have we any details?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Well it has several fire-spitting maws, allegedly, which troublingly fits the profile of your father's household pet. Though, if this really is the case... may it simply let you pass!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: What, you mean Cerberus himself guards the entrance to Tartarus? Why would he do the bidding of Chronos? Impossible.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: A word of caution, Goddess, in regard to an impediment you may confront when finally you reach the far end of the Mourning Fields. Reports suggest it's some sort of infernal beast... a big one. Our Shades had to stay very far away.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I can corroborate our Shades' reports. And add that this infernal beast is none other than Cerberus, my father's household pet... acting on instinct and pure rage.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Ahh, I feared as much. For they said the beast had several fire-spitting maws. I don't suppose he recognized you after all this time, and... let you pass...?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Our Shades have analyzed your clashes against Cerberus. He's more vulnerable from the back, although if you have to get close, don't stay there for too long.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: That makes sense, as he's immensely strong... not the fastest foe I've stood against, but I don't feel as though I can confront him head on.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Well, don't get too far away from him either, as that's when he'll attempt to breath his flames. He must have some weakness to exploit; not like he's fighting you strategically.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I must admit, on some occasion all the tales you told about the sea-monster Charybdis felt almost beyond belief. Now having faced the blasted thing, I feel you sold it short.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: What, faced Charybdis? Ah, Goddess. I'd heard reports about some sort of whirlpool within Poseidon's Rift and now my fear's confirmed. At least she didn't swallow you outright...!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I must admit, on some occasion all the tales you told about the sea-monster Charybdis felt almost beyond belief. Now having faced the blasted thing, I feel you sold it short.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: What, faced Charybdis? Ah, Goddess. I'd heard reports about some sort of whirlpool within Poseidon's Rift and now my fear's confirmed. At least she didn't swallow you outright...!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Perhaps I could have offered more-creative ways of navigating past Charybdis in your voyages when last we spoke of her. I realize sailing around may not be possible...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: No, it is not. I thought the main threat was to fall into that maw of hers... but I've found that her many thrashing arms or tentacles present more of a problem.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Then worry about them. Perhaps you could isolate a couple at a time, use whatever space you have to keep moving. Poke her in the arms enough and she ought to back off.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: We've multiple reports the armies of Chronos are massing on the surface up there, and not yet caught wind of your efforts. At least you aren't contending with them, aye?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: My route through the Underworld is fraught in its own right, but yes, it's comforting to know that army of traitors remains ignorant of me.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: There's not a downward route that's more discreet. Those thorny chaps in the woods, they've no love for Chronos either, at least.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Got a most troubling report, a sighting in the pathways on the outskirts of Erebus. Did our eyes out there see true?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: If it's Chronos that they saw, then yes. Now at least I know what he looks like; but he knows me, in turn. Why do you think he let me go? Have we been compromised?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: No one gets into the Crossroads whom we don't permit, or can even know we're here... not even him. So he's sniffing around? Fine. He wastes his time whilst you get closer to your mark.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: You look as though you've seen a ghost, if you'll forgive the very antiquated mortal turn-of-phrase, Goddess. So we'd say to someone looking disconcerted. Things all right?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Yes and no; I saw Chronos out there. I thought he had me, but I broke free from his spell. This was in Oceanus, though I also caught him snooping near the pathways out of Erebus.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Damn it... our reports said nothing of the sort, but then... some of our Shade informants haven't yet returned. We had expected Chronos to stay put in the lowest reaches. You must have caused enough of a stir to catch his notice.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I've hardly been discreet in my approach. Is there truly no other way?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: We reckon not. We're counting on him thinking you're inconsequential to his plans. As for our own, I shall go over this with the good witch. Be safe, and cheers for the report.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: You found him, Goddess... your look says it all, along with my reports. Gave him something to remember you by, I reckon, even if the battle didn't go your way...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Chronos was exactly where we expected... but I could not foresee his every technique. And my ability to withstand his magick went only so far.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Well, fortunately for you, the old are often rather slow to learn. You run into him a few more times like that, you'll soon know every trick he's got. Though... do be very careful out there, aye?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Our Shades have proved unable to approach the House of Hades, so they've no reports about the way the Titan fights. I'm afraid you're on your own for that one, Goddess...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Oh I can give you a report myself. He looks rather frail, but he's quick. His scythe has significant reach, and he can freeze me at a moment's notice, but only for so long. And his forces are present to ensure I'm outnumbered.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Freeze you... meaning he tries to stop you using time, but can't hold you for long. Just as the good witch thought then, aye? It means his power over you is limited; you're the one that can't be stopped, not him.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: You got him, didn't you? Killed the bastard, for... one night, at least. The first report I heard, I scarce believed, but by the third or fourth, I'd come around. Well done, Goddess!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I'd not be surprised if our reports already say he's returned. But I got what I needed for now. And a little bit of payback, I suppose.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Aye, and he'll be sore about it, I expect, but... even though we're not there yet... we needed this, I think. You needed this. On to the next phase then?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: During a recent voyage, I found Circe the Enchantress on her little isle, evidently safe from all the turmoil at sea. Don't you two know each other fairly well?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: In life and death we can but strive to know each other well, Goddess.... Though I knew Circe... just a bit, at least; as casual acquaintances is all, why do you ask?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Oh just in case you wanted me to tell her anything when next we met, as she may be providing some assistance when I navigate the Rift of Thessaly.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: No, no need to tell her anything. Best keep your focus on the task at hand.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Odysseus, what happened between you and Circe, anyhow? She mentioned you, said nothing in particular, except... I sensed some pain. There and here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: It's not a pain entirely, Goddess. When I was whole, returning from a war, I found Circe and her isle, and... I lived there for about a year. She housed me and my men, and we grew close. I learned a lot from her, and then... we left.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Your voyage home. You stopped for an entire year, then voyaged on and never saw her again...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Well, aye, until I saw her again here. It's just... it's in the past, and for the both of us, I think. Not every such relationship need last eternally.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: That renewal project that we used to talk about? I still think of it all the time, these Crossroads flourishing with worldly delights...!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I still think about it, too, and made some preliminary steps of late. The well-being of every last Shade in this place is important to our goal.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: But, we've more pressing matters now, it seems. What, you intend to stop fighting Chronos just to spruce up the surroundings here?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: You've recruited that one shifty-looking Shade for the renewal project! A scrappy start is still a start, I'm... pleased to see it underway!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I thought you might say that! It gives Dora something practical to do. And means the project needn't be a hopeless dream.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Note we have several projects to expand these Crossroads that could use your blessings when you get the opportunity, Goddess. Your choice, and there's no urgency at all.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Eventually the waters in the hot springs and the fishing pier need to be purified, not to mention the taverna grounds. Have you a preferential order between those?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Well you know the Shades, they like to gather up in the taverna, so I reckon I'd start there... or whichever one is simplest for you!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Did you ever face Dracons in your mortal days, Odysseus? The kind that fly about on wings much like a bat's, but huge. They've become quite a problem on Olympus recently.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: My home was mountainous though we were mostly free from flying beasts. Still, in my experience they all have something in common: Susceptibility to being shot out of the sky.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Did you ever face Dracons in your mortal days, Odysseus? The kind that fly about on wings much like a bat's, but huge. They've become quite a problem on Olympus recently.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: My home was mountainous though we were mostly free from flying beasts. Still, in my experience they all have something in common: Susceptibility to being shot out of the sky.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Those thorny chaps you've been dealing with... their numbers grow in each report I get. If you've thoughts on how we could have better prepared you...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I'll share my thoughts when necessary, Odysseus. For the moment, I'm sorting through them still.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Aye, best not to speak without prepared remarks. I understand the feeling only too well. You'll keep me posted, though?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Have you caught Eris snooping through our plans or anything? Have to be even more discreet with her about...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: She stays over in her little corner mostly, Goddess, but I've been keeping my eye on her in case of anything. As long as she undermines our efforts well out of earshot...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Good, you're back. According to reports, Eris flew off toward the Rift of Thessaly on an intercept course with the fleet. Except I don't think that she's headed there to help. Not you, at least.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: These reports aren't of much benefit to me after the fact. But at least I can confirm they're accurate. How preposterous that we cannot rid ourselves of her.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: All we can do is work with what we've got. I apologize that we could not forewarn you on this one. We'll continue investigating her motives and accomplices.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Eris making problems for you on the surface, isn't she... whilst so close to your goal. Well if it's any reassurance, we know the workings of her weapon better than she does. She must run out of ammunition frequently...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: She does. Sometimes she jams the mechanism, gets distracted getting it unstuck. Should use those opportunities to strike. Not much place for taking cover anyhow.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: She's trying to intimidate you. With the Rail of Adamant, she's much more dangerous at range. Perhaps you could outflank or outmaneuver her somehow...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Word has it Eris has been spotted in the depths along your path... intelligence you likely could have used before she made your journey worse for you. Apologies, Goddess...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: She's trying to stop me, or at least slow me down. And... she knows too much about our intentions. What do we do? Children of Nyx are supposed to be on our side.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: She doesn't know the half of what we've planned. Besides, her boredom drives her more than anything. Run into her a few more times, she'll soon grow sick of it and go away.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Hey, where'd Hecuba and Gale scamper off to? I'd ask Headmistress but she doesn't like it when I show even the slightest concern for her Familiars. They're fine, Melinoë.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh they probably are fine! Each on a little scouting expedition at my own behest, under the auspices of the good witch of course. The hound goes one way, the polecat another.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Those little scouting expeditions always seem to last a while. I'll have to keep an eye out for them, then.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Ah, you made it to the Mourning Fields, good! Tartarus is not too far behind... directly underneath, in fact, but the ingress is a bit of a walk from your initial drop...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: A bit of a walk, fraught with soul-devouring Daemons and other life-hating fiends. Too powerful to be imprisoned so they're free to wander there, something like that?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: We mortals just love the Fields, Goddess! A place to rid oneself of misery — or drown in it. A perfect spot for Daemons, only some of which shall leave you be.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: What was the source of your misfortune, that you're back with such a scowl? Some Wailers, or Caskets, perhaps? Oh... a Shadow-Spiller.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Yes, a Shadow-Spiller. I lost my way against one. Can hardly see a thing when they're about! I should be stronger than this...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: You've strength enough. They merely test your nerve. Foes such as that are to be faced head-on, no hesitation. Set for another go?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Our Record Keeper there once worked in a secure Administrative Chamber in your father's halls, if you can believe it, Goddess. Must have been quite the experience!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: My father must have prized such analytical minds as that one's. Perhaps once all of this is over, the Record Keeper could return to working in my father's House.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: I'd floated that idea myself, to which the Record Keeper cowered, for some reason, and refused to talk to me again! Bit of an odd one, aye?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Your little garden there has cheered this old place up a bit, Goddess! In trying times, such simple pleasures oft are first to fade. Precisely when they're needed most.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Thank you, and pleased to hear it, Great Tactician. Though, I'd not expected a mere flower patch to leave such an impression on someone like you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Come now, all I do is pay attention to details. Victory comes to those whose hearts are predisposed. I mean, look at us, our spirits are quite literally all we have; and now, flowers!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Not the report I wished to deliver, though I've some idea of what Chronos has been doing with all of his excess Gold... I was trounced last night by a gigantic bag of coins.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: A Goldwrath, then! I reckon if you're Chronos, and you've got a limitless supply of shiny metal... might as well use some of them for your profane research. Ah, but you were close...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You've known Headmistress quite a while, haven't you? Where did you meet, I always wanted to ask?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Heh, well. All I can say is that I was... alone. At the end of one life, and the start of another. Lost. She appeared before me then, in all her splendor, and... she revealed to me a path I hadn't seen.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: That all sounds very figurative. Were you already dead?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: I was. She told me she could grant me a new life. She needed a tactician knowledgeable of the surface. I thought, who am I to turn a goddess down? I followed her. It's been a while now.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: You know the tricks of the good witch by now, don't you? Or has she held to some of them so that your testing can be more of a surprise?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Her Triple Divide is hard to deal with... mirror images that can't be touched but can hit me just fine. Then her Sisters of the Dead render her entirely immune...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Well, do teach those witch-followers a lesson soon as they appear, though you didn't hear it from me. You'll figure her out. She wants you to succeed.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Not seen the good witch recently, have you, Goddess? You'd tell me if there was any reason for concern...?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I encountered mighty Heracles on the surface. No mistaking him! Though I thought he was dead. He seemed very much alive, on the outside, anyway. And still in service to the gods...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: The great Heracles... that's a complicated one. Part man, part god... part monster too, perhaps. The rules of death don't quite apply to him. What did he want? He give you any trouble?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: He was quite brusque. Told me to stay out of his way. Sounds like he has a task of his own. Maybe Olympus isn't counting on me to succeed...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Eh, don't take it personally. You have wise relatives, and ruthless ones. They may not like their odds hinging on any one individual. And... do watch yourself with Heracles, aye?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: So, there's a bit of a new wrinkle in our plans. We've been able to verify some of the details that Master Hermes suggested to you, about what's... brewing on the surface.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Please tell me what you may... and if you think it wise for me to deviate from my objective to address whatever's going on up there.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: The forces of Chronos gather to sail on Olympus through a channel cut across the land. You're poised to disrupt the enemy ranks from within. Aid Olympus, and your chances against Chronos may improve.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: The warded pathway leading up should drop me right into the heart of our foe's forward garrison. It seems so reckless...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Odysseus, have you ever seen Hypnos so much as shift in his sleep? I know he's been through a lot, but still...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Can't say I have! Been just that way from the night the good witch got him. The snoring I became accustomed to eventually...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Think he's ever going to wake? None of the incantations we've tried have had any effect.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: So blasted quiet now... at first I couldn't tell what changed, till I recalled Hypnos had gone away. I was so accustomed to always hearing him in the background...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Wait, you miss his ceaseless snoring? You of everyone at the Crossroads were in closest proximity, so I figured you'd be the most pleased that he's finally home.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Pleased for him, aye, and the auditory change isn't a bad one, but... even when things improve, it can be an adjustment.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: I take it you're the reason Icarus is back. Always seemed like a good lad! I kept my mouth shut at the time but I was disappointed when he left.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: It was his choice to make. I think he always valued your experience as well. He didn't get to have adventures like you back when he was alive.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Who does, Goddess? Though clearly he picked up some know-how whilst away. Even got wings to show for it! Think he might have an extra set?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Have the old Knuckle Bones been of some use, Goddess? Must look a little worn, but... brought me fortune many times, in games of chance and when my life was on the line.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Odysseus, if they were dear to you all through your life, why would you give them to me now? No matter how useful they still may be, which is quite a bit!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Come now, nothing would please me more than for you to succeed. If some small token of my past could be of benefit, it's yours. Besides, I got a Nectar bottle in exchange!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Can you confirm a sighting of Queen Lamia down in the Mourning Fields, Goddess? I'd caution you to watch for signs of slithering about, except I reckon I may be too late...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I must have drawn her out of her den, yes. That or I barged into it, but either way, her magick proved too much for me last night...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Anything I can do for you of late, Goddess? Such a feeling, when our work at last is more or less complete. Rather, the work itself is never-ending, but at least some of the pressure's gone for now.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: There's always the self-imposed pressure! But you're right, it's not been quite as daunting recently. As for what you can do... let's just keep talking when we're here!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: The good witch put you to the test out there, did she? Quite an intensive teaching style, I always thought. She expects a lot from you, but that's just what happens when you've such potential.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Then she must be disappointed with my results, although she's careful not to show it. Same as you, Odysseus.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Come, I know how dispiriting it is to chart one's course, only to lose one's way. We rarely get where we must go the fastest way we can.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: See Charon's latest haul, Goddess? Shipment by the riverside, all yours. And we're watching the rivers, making certain this won't be the last.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Lord Charon risks aiding my efforts in such a direct way. Are we able to compensate him at all?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Aye, with what Gold we've gathered recently. He really means to melt down every last one of those Chronos coins we can get our hands on.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: You must be Charon's finest customer. I reckon he enjoys the frequent visits to the Underworld, in his way. At least it's mostly cargo shipments now, not just confused, complaining Shades of the recently deceased...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Did you accept your death with dignity when it was finally your time, Odysseus? You seem as though you would have clung to life with all your might.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, I'd gotten on in years by then. Still didn't care much for the circumstances, not that anybody does! Though, I did appreciate the opportunity to meet the fearsome Boatman at long last, and ride into the Underworld the proper way.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You've mentioned that you'd gone into the Underworld and back while you still lived. Lord Charon didn't take you then?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh! Hail, Goddess, I, erm... didn't notice your approach. Half-inclined to blame the onset of the years, but I've no such excuses in this state.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I didn't mean to startle you. How's the mood been around here, everything all right?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Mood and everything is well enough. Our ranks swell with Shades moved by the tale that the Princess of the Underworld has returned, with vengeance on her mind.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Nothing like a bit of sworn vengeance to inspire the masses. Though, what about you?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Everything's shipshape with me; especially after a brief reprieve right here. Be well, Goddess.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: So Doom has come to stay with us a bit? My last and only other brush with him did not resolve particularly well, so... I'll just keep my distance, if that's quite all right.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: All children of Nyx are welcome in these Crossroads. Though I think Lord Moros wishes to keep his distance, too.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Our good witch there knows you've a mountain of a journey ahead of you, Goddess. You hear about our mountains on the surface? Big tall rocks?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: What, you mean like Mount Olympus, or Mount Othrys? Of course I've heard of them! They reach up to the sky. I can only imagine.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: You look up from the base to the peak, you'll swear they can't be climbed, but! If you only concern yourself with making that next step... you'll make it to the top.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: It's grown much livelier now with the Music Maker here! A fine choice of recruit for us, Goddess. I was always partial to a rousing battle-ballad, though a variety is welcome, too.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I figured that the Music Maker would grow rather popular quite soon. Got any song requests?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh you don't want to know; happy for you to make the hard decisions there!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Nemesis has been in an especially foul mood of late. Anything I should know about?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I was going to ask you the same. She sought my task, but that's nothing new. Though, she's been clashing with Headmistress more and more...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Ah, shame it's been this way. Personally, I'd rather have Divine Retribution with me than against. I reckon you can't just smooth things over with her, can you?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: So, how has Nemesis been faring out there? Taking matters into her very capable hands. Not making too much grief for you, I reckon?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: So you know already? And Headmistress must, too. Nemesis is being Nemesis! Discovering firsthand our task is not as simple as it seems.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Say no more, then! Though, if she becomes too intent on impeding your results over achieving her own, we'll step in.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: You seem to have the situation well in hand of late, Goddess. Do let me know if you need anything, though I reckon the nights when you required Psyche or Ashes and such are behind us.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Odysseus, I need your presence here more than I need your resource offerings, though those were often very helpful, too. Let's keep this up, shall we?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: The good witch there noted you were able to vanquish her in a full-on contest. Impressive stuff! You get much farther?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I did. Down to the chambers near the bottom of the sea. Strange music echoing all through the place. I wasn't prepared.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Strange music, huh... reminds me of when... oh, I'll spare the tales for another time! You've enough on your mind, I can tell.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: It's swarming season for the Hellifish in Oceanus, as you may have seen. Should you run into them, advice is not to bother much with smaller ones but get the biggest one.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I'd rather not bother with any of them and all their stinging. How long do swarming seasons typically go on?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Well, season is a bit of a misnomer, as the current season started back when I was just a lad. The good news is you get accustomed to the stinging after a while!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: It's swarming season for the Hellifish in Oceanus, as you may have seen. Should you run into them, advice is not to bother much with smaller ones but get the biggest one.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I'd rather not bother with any of them and all their stinging. How long do swarming seasons typically go on?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Well, season is a bit of a misnomer, as the current season started back when I was just a lad. The good news is you get accustomed to the stinging after a while!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: This may not be the best occasion to inform you but we've reason to believe the trouble brewing on Olympus is significantly worse than it appears. Not even the gods are fully aware. Unfortunately, neither are we...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: The forces of Chronos attack nonstop, while the gods and their defenses hold them back... then it's a ruse for some especially dramatic gesture that the Titan has in store?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: My thoughts exactly. But have no fear, Goddess! I once fought alongside a great warrior who said fear is for the weak. I happen to think fear is for the living, but like most good things, it isn't healthy in abundance.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: How was the surface, still warm and welcoming I reckon? You're to be commended for even setting foot up there.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: It was dreadful. The city of Ephyra is completely overrun. And, there's a blind Cyclops making sure no one gets out.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Well, that all sounds entirely unfortunate. That blind Cyclops must be Polyphemus. My crew and I once had a bad run-in with him. He still going on about eating people?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Your old adversary Polyphemus wanted me to give you a message on his behalf. Though, come to think, I'm uncertain what he wanted me to say. Perhaps just... kick you savagely?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Eh, sounds like the Cyclops that I used to know all right. My crew and I, we were his captives for a while... and his food supply. I found a way to get us out of there, but it was ugly...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: He calls you Nobody. How did he come to know you by that name?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Nobody took his eye, Goddess... I gave to him a name that wouldn't make a lick of sense when he called over to his mates for help. We had to try.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: How is old Polyphemus, anyhow? Slowed down a bit since he was young, perhaps...? I scarce was any match for him, but I was merely mortal, whilst you're... you.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: He's territorial, though seems much more concerned with sleep and appetite than any other cause. So, you really blinded him, then...?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: I did... and only wish he had another eye so that I could have done it twice. For evidently he is not remorseful in the least for what he did, and... as you plainly see, neither am I.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: The Cyclops giving you some trouble, isn't he? Must have learned to overcome his now-deficient sight and use his other senses to keep track of you.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: He hits extremely hard, and not only is able to smell me somehow, but I think may even sense my every step. How did you manage to defeat him?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Got him in his sleep. Not an honorable fight but wasn't a fair situation, as Nemesis herself would surely agree. In your case I'd jab at him and not over-commit, wear him down!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: With your Olympus-dwelling relatives assisting you, I've half a mind to ask you to let them know I'm on your side, except... not all of them love me.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: What? But you're the Great Tactician, Odysseus! Even the gods were impressed with your machinations in your glory days.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Lady Athena and others favored me, it's true, but your uncle Poseidon, well... I got on his bad side, I believe. Maybe he's let it go after all this time but that's a risk I'm not prepared to take, so erm, keep quiet about me, would you?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: How did you manage it, Odysseus? Back when you had the outcome of a full-scale war riding on you, then a treacherous sea-journey, all with only one life to live... or so then you thought?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: For certain, the one-life-to-live bit instilled in me the will to survive. Most mortals cling to life, but I was better than most at rejecting my doubts. And when my plans all fell apart, I improvised...!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Your plans fell apart? How did you avoid becoming distraught? Keep all your doubts at bay in the face of failure?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: It's that I always relished finding a way out. No matter what I'd gotten myself into. Treating my predicaments as mazes to be navigated, puzzles to be solved...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: But at times there are impasses; outcomes that simply cannot be achieved...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: You asked before about how I managed it... the pressure to always prevail through what, in retrospect, were... many terribly exciting scenarios, all whilst bearing the burden of so many others' expectations and my own.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You said you always believed there was a solution, a positive outcome that could be discovered and achieved... I've been thinking about that.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Good. But it occurred to me there's something else: Love drove me on, Goddess. The great war that earned me my place in Elysium... I wanted desperately not to go. To stay home with my wife and newborn son.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You were left with no choice but to fight. So you held to hope that someday you'd see your family again...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Prometheus opposes us... now that one hurts, Goddess. Amongst us mortals, he was quite popular. Beloved, even! Gave us the gift of fire. Practically made us who we are.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: And in so doing, he violated the will of Olympus. Supposedly he has the gift of foresight... he ought to have anticipated the consequences of his disobedience.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: What makes you think he didn't? All the more reason we admired him. He knew the cost of what he did for us, and paid it willingly, or... so I choose to think. But all that's in the past. If he's thrown in with Chronos now, he's lost his way.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Prometheus opposes us... now that one hurts, Goddess. Amongst us mortals, he was quite popular. Beloved, even! Gave us the gift of fire. Practically made us who we are.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: And in so doing, he violated the will of Olympus. Supposedly he has the gift of foresight... he ought to have anticipated the consequences of his disobedience.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: What makes you think he didn't? All the more reason we admired him. He knew the cost of what he did for us, and paid it willingly, or... so I choose to think. But all that's in the past. If he's thrown in with Chronos now, he's lost his way.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Wanted to ask you more about Prometheus. By your own estimation, is he truly mad...? And, if he stands opposed to all your family, then who set him loose to begin with...?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I don't exactly know on either count. He speaks in riddles sometimes, but he doesn't sound as though he's lost his mind. My suspicion is that the forces of Chronos liberated him in exchange for his obedience, but...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You know mortals better than anybody here. Prometheus claims that the gods are cruel, and that he fights not for the Titan Lord, but mortalkind. What do you make of all that?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Look, I've known mortals who think gods can be uncaring. Some of them defy the gods even in death. Others understand that gods are... everything, really. We have only this world, more beautiful than it is flawed.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You're speaking in the abstract. I want to know your personal opinion here, Odysseus. Are the gods in the wrong? Did we bring this on ourselves...?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: My personal opinion is that gods and mortals are a complicated bunch; and that you shouldn't doubt yourself, lest you falter. If I felt you or the good witch were in the wrong, I'd tell you so.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Prometheus persists in what he's doing, aye? Our Shades have seen him on the mountain still.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Yes, he's there, leading remnants of the Titan's legions... Grandfather's legions. All part of the conditions allowing me to face the remaining possibilities of Typhon.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: He couldn't have possibly foreseen all this... could he?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Odysseus, I need your counsel and discretion on something. Prometheus has an ally whom I thought was on my family's side. I faced them together near the mountain's summit, where the smoke is thick. But, I mustn't say who...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Mm, quite the report all right, and I have my suspicions as to whom you mean. Certainly the gods must know, at least Master Hermes, or... do you reckon that they're unaware?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I don't think they realize, though perhaps Hermes does and won't let on. Prometheus said I wouldn't tell a soul who it was because of the harm that doing so would bring. Do you think he's telling the truth?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: I'd not wager otherwise. We had best treat it as the truth for now, unless you believe this ally poses an imminent threat to your family. Are you in a position to further investigate?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: What news if any of the matter with Prometheus you had mentioned before? We've received no reports that match what you said... which I take as a positive sign.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: It was likely my own presence that revealed this hidden ally from the smoke and shadows. Those two must be keeping one another informed... about both Chronos and the gods.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Aye, having informants on the opposing side is one of the oldest tricks of the trade. You're still not planning to expose who this ally is?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Ah, I miss the feeling of the rain, sometimes. Then I remember years at sea, soaked to the bone, and thank the gods the blasted stuff can't get to me as once it did.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: The rain brings life, as well as death... especially the latter on this night. The Lost Souls out there seem to hate it near as much as you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Aye, almost seems to soften them up, doesn't it? Leave them ripe for the harvest. Go pick some off for me, will you?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: They sent the Verminancer after you, Goddess? We know that Satyr by no other title, nor of any others of that size. He's one of the Titan Lord's high priests!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Then the Titan Lord shall soon have one fewer high priest.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Even if you take him, our reports say that he cannot truly die. A gift from Chronos, in exchange for performing the ritual that brought him back...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: We missed you here, and I'm so pleased you're back! How was your trip? Did you find your family?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Aye, Goddess, I found them all right. Followed a couple leads down in Elysium, ran into a couple of former compatriots, and we got there. Penelope and Telemachus... erm, well, they recognized me right away.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Were they all right? Apologies, it's not my wish to drag these details out of you, it's just... this is your latest tale!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Ahh, what's to tell? It was... well, it was comforting. I reckon the time we spent apart was healing in a way. They asked I visit them again, down the line.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: That older-looking Shade that joined us with those baubles and whatnot... some sort of oracle or sage, aye? What an unusual vocation, to be a chronicler of history.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: The wizened Sage remembers each and every night since all of this began. Every victory and setback, now available for my review.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Eugh... I wouldn't look upon my life in such a way. But, to each their own! And I reckon it's a fine means to learn from your mistakes if you've the stomach for that sort of thing.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: You've gone at this for fifty nights at least, Goddess. Is there occasion yet to give yourself a bit of a reprieve? You're putting yourself through a lot is all.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I've had reprieve along the way, thank you. The springs, the garden, the taverna... even just conversing like this! It's not as though I'm pushing myself in every waking moment.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Aye, we never can push ourselves as hard as possible for as long as we'd like. But, you've come a long way, and in the off chance you're not keeping track, I am!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: This is about your hundredth night, Goddess... several moons at least in mortal terms. Feels like a while, yet no time at all. That's the insidious work of Chronos for you!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I think you're right, now that you mention it! The passage of time has always felt strange to me, but even more so since this all began. Did you feel similarly in your mortal days?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Aye, like you have no idea. It must be even stranger for you gods... so much time can pass in the blink of an eye! The best we all can do is use it well.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: According to our counts, you have prevailed on more than fifty nights now, Goddess. Wanted to take a moment to celebrate that achievement with you.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Has it already been that long? Time works in strange ways.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: It's been five hundred nights since all of this began, Goddess. Well, more or less. In mortal terms... that's quite a while. Perhaps even for you.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I ceased keeping track at a certain point, and relinquished any coherent sense of the passage of time. When do you suppose we ought to wind down on all this?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Not sighted Chronos on the surface yet. Why risk it when you've got bloodthirsty Satyrs serving as your eyes and ears? His forces are more organized than first they seemed.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I just had an unfortunate run-in with one of those Satyrs. Well-armed, well-trained... and Chronos has entire legions.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Hrm, this is the war they've sought ever since the gods put Chronos in the ground. The promise of the Titan's return; immortality and untold riches...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: How's life of late down at the bottom of the sea? All sorts of nastiness lurking there, that the reports make clear. I wager you've seen some things.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You'd win that wager handily. The worst of them has to be this sea-monstress called Scylla, and her Siren friends. They have this ridiculous act...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: What, Scylla's still around? With the Sirens, no less, hah! It's a wonder you made it back. Please tell me you didn't listen to a word they sang?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: I once was desperate to hear the Sirens' song... I barely resisted their temptations; of course I was... merely mortal. Perhaps you're less susceptible to their allure.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Perhaps. At any rate I fail to see the appeal. Although, it's possible the Sirens were better off without Scylla. She seems rather bossy...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh she's a menace. Caught half a dozen of my crew like she'd a separate set of teeth for each of the poor lads. We'd little choice but to pray to the gods, and sail on. Tell her hello for me!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Did the gods answer your call at least?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I've seen you here alone more often lately, Odysseus. You're thinking about your family, aren't you? Some potent memory from your past.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Ah, Goddess, you may see through most of us Shades, but your empathy for me's a bit... misplaced. Penelope, Telemachus, and I... we're cordial with each other still, but... how to say this without sounding callous...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Please speak your mind with me. I'm certain you're not callous.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: ...My mind wanders not to matters of family, but... to the Sirens' blasted song. I scarce got to hear it even once, whilst navigating through their waters. I wanted desperately to cast myself into the sea, just for a better listen...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: The Sirens' song? You can't expect me to believe that Scylla's incessant drivel is the source of the Great Tactician's concerns of late?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Now, now, spare me the ridicule, I'm dead serious about this. You weren't with me on the surface there and wouldn't understand...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: When last we spoke in the taverna, I didn't mean to shame you for your musical tastes. I just had no idea that Scylla could be taken seriously by anybody... much less you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Ah, think nothing of it. Siren music's an acquired taste... acquired by all those who sailed in their vicinity whilst I was whole. Incessant drivel, though, that's all you think it is?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Well, perhaps I was a bit uncharitable in my assessment, it's just... I have trouble separating my disdain for the performer from the quality of her craft.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Were you aware that Scylla and Charybdis had teamed up?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh you could certainly say that. Why do you ask?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I caught their latest performance. It was quite a spectacle to say the least. You could have forewarned me that the two were working in concert!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Goddess, forgive me, but I thought it was quite common knowledge that Scylla and Charybdis were collaborating... it's not a new development at all.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: That music... I'd recognize it anywhere! Even without the words. And here I thought I must have been the sole admirer of Siren songs in all the Crossroads.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I... just wanted to see if our Music Maker even knew that particular piece. Perhaps it shouldn't be permitted here. Especially if it has insidious power over men's hearts.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh I think we dead men are no longer as susceptible. Though I'd best give it another listen, just to be certain.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Scylla mentioned something about a potential sponsor in the audience of one of her shows, Odysseus. Do you have any idea who that could be? I thought it might be Chronos, but can't imagine him listening to her music.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: A potential sponsor... that's not come up in our reports, though I'll review and let you know if anything turns up. Probably just some old sailor who got an earful and couldn't let it go. Do you know what this sponsorship entails?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Sirens proving to be quite the hindrance, aren't they. Our Shades have intermingled with the crowd of onlookers surrounding them, and have some observations to impart.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: About what, my repeated failures to overcome a deranged musical troupe despite intensive lifelong training in both magickal and martial ways?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: No need to stand in your own way, Goddess. With Scylla and the Sirens, you are up against three foes at once. The percussionist is stuck in place but presents a significant threat. Our Shades suggest dealing with that one first.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You and Commander Schelemeus both were great and famous sailors, isn't that so? Did the two of you ever meet in your mortal days?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, great sailor is an understatement, Goddess! They say his exploits rivaled even mine! Alas that I never once had an opportunity to witness his prowess. Ah, it must have been something!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Why not send Nemesis to the surface in my stead? Even if I undo the wards sealing the path, I don't know that I'll last long up there.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: The matter on Olympus isn't as personal for Nemesis as it is for you; and she hasn't been especially receptive to suggestion recently.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: The good witch always figured you would make your way up to the mortal realm. Once you were ready and the situation called for it. Well done.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: She warded the pathway there, until such time as I had the means to pass through. But the climate on the surface is another matter...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Your Underworld birthright stuff getting in the way? A curse not just a blessing, it would seem. Well, if your witchery got you this far, surely it can aid with this... predicament.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: What brought you back that it would cause you visible distress, Goddess? We heard you made it to the summit but reports are inconclusive after that... or, unbelievable.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Some sort of infestation plagues the summit... monstrosities abound. They can't possibly be servants of Chronos, but... they must be part of his scheme.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Well let's not speculate before we gather all the necessary facts. You've stood against these monstrosities and managed to return. Your family members must know more, provided they're... still able to reach you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: This new table's excellent! Plenty of room for our map of all the regions that we're monitoring, and these side compartments for our new reports, and an elegant finish...!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Odysseus, somehow I'd not expected you to have this much enthusiasm for a piece of furnishing. Especially a piece you strictly need to carry out your duties here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Well, that's quite an accusation... coming from someone who seems to love her work at least as much as I. Or perhaps you've just never had a proper table to your name?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You know the great Olympian defender, Talos? One of the most ingenious inventions of Hephaestus, and a walking work of art... yet utterly unable to distinguish friend from foe.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: That disappointing lack of judgment allegedly is not an oversight. Olympus deploys Talos where they want no one to tread, and lately, their own mountain is that place.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Sometimes I think Olympus doesn't have the most cohesive plan for their defense. Isn't Lady Athena herself supposed to be in charge?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Supposed to be, aye. You know your family, Goddess! In matters of importance, they all want to get involved.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: I heard your efforts to bring Cerberus back to his senses were successful... at least temporarily, Goddess. He bounded off to Tartarus, only to be cast out into the Fields again?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: That's what I gathered, yes. In Tartarus, he seeks his master... my father. Whom I happened upon, bound in chains! Chronos is torturing them... mocking them.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Chronos is using them to get at you. Instill in you emotions that compel you to throw caution to the wind. Remember, wars are won and lost in hearts and minds.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Did you not act on your emotions in your mortal days?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Your pathway down into the Underworld may seem a little roundabout, but avoiding the main regions is your best shot at eluding Chronos till you can face him on your terms.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I have that pathway long since memorized; a straight shot down to Tartarus would have been too good to be true. I'm grateful that this other route even exists.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: And I am grateful that your father's realm is vast indeed, that we could chart a course along its border all the way to where you need to be.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: How was it sailing through the Rift of Thessaly, Goddess? Sometimes I long for my past days and nights at sea. Probably since I've forgotten just how miserable it was...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I feel I could remind you at this point. Chronos has this vast fleet of ships drawn from the very depths, drowned crews and all. I had to commandeer whichever craft I could.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: A proper nautical experience, then! Fraught with danger, mystery...! Ah, I feel a little envious of those drowned crews! After I died, I thought my sailing days were done.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Well, Chronos could probably use more sailors after what I did to them.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Goddess, I was just thinking of your travels, taking you beneath the surface and above, I reckon to the ends of the earth! No mere journey... got to be a better word for it...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: What, is task not sufficiently extravagant? Perhaps assignment? Or voyage? Quest doesn't feel right at all...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: No, too upbeat, and voyage is more of a nautical term.... Erm, we'll think of something.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: When was the last reported sighting of a Root-Stalker out in the woods?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: See, that's the sort of inquiry I'd expect from somebody who sighted a Root-Stalker out in the woods very, very recently. So there's still something rotten in Erebus, then?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: You've Typhon to contend with on that mountaintop, Goddess...? Our Shades say he's near the size of a mountain himself... please tell me that's hyperbole on their part?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Well to put it in perspective... by my estimation, he's at least a hundred times the size of Polyphemus. Though I've not had the opportunity to take his measure properly.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: You'll take his measure yet, I reckon. It's what you've done with every other foe you've faced thus far! Our Shades and the good witch think it unwise to take on such a threat yourself, but... you're not planning to back down, are you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: You've Typhon to contend with on that mountaintop, Goddess...? Our Shades say he's near the size of a mountain himself... please tell me that's hyperbole on their part?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Well to put it in perspective... by my estimation, he's more than a hundred times the size of a Cyclops. Though I've not had the opportunity to take his measure properly.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: You'll take his measure yet, I reckon. It's what you've done with every other foe you've faced thus far! Our Shades and the good witch think it unwise to take on such a threat yourself, but... you're not planning to back down, are you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: How are you holding up, Goddess...? My instinct is to celebrate the death of Typhon, but... I never forgot the first man I slew. How the light left his eyes...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Typhon sought destruction, and he got it. I won't rejoice in the outcome, but won't lament it either. Especially when there's more left to be done.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: You have the right approach. You'll have time to recount every choice and consequence when all of this is over, but for now... best to press on and finish what you started.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: The good witch has been quite concerned about your confrontations with Typhon. Reckon you never trained to take opponents of that size.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Oh I trained just last night. Typhon is immensely strong, and large enough to blot out the light of the Moon, but evidently not invincible. They say Lord Zeus defeated him before.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: They say a lot of things, Goddess; I'm far more interested in your account. If it's your instinct that this monster can be stopped, your word is good enough for me. Though I would... also love to see some proof, of course.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Which sorts of foul monstrosities were the Olympians contending with of late? As if the Titan's armies weren't troublesome enough, now Typhon's brood...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Well... imagine horrors of all shapes and sizes, each some mix of muscle, eyes, and teeth. They climb up from beneath the ground... some practically ooze from the walls! The one that got me last night was particularly large.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: And I take it Typhon has far more of these children than even your Lord Uncle Zeus, and all spoiled rotten. Feuding families! The cause of most every great war I've known.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Which sorts of foul monstrosities were the Olympians contending with of late? As if the Titan's armies weren't troublesome enough, now Typhon's brood...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Well... imagine horrors of all shapes and sizes, each some mix of muscle, eyes, and teeth. They climb up from beneath the ground... some practically ooze from the walls!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: And I take it that he has far more of these children than even your Lord Uncle Zeus, and all spoiled rotten. Feuding families! Cause of most every great war I've known.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: You felled that mountain-sized monstrosity, Goddess...! It would be false of me to say I had no doubt you would, though... I'd only a bit, I swear. So, how did you outsmart him?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Outsmart him? All I could do was keep hitting him with all my might. It helped that Lord Zeus also struck him hard with lightning bolts. Eventually it worked, and Typhon fell.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Well! Your battle prowess may be unsurpassed, but your ability to weave a yarn is... not quite there. When next you overcome the Father of All Monsters, do make of it a much more rousing tale, would you?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: So there you were! Alone, atop the rain-soaked mountain... when out of the dark came a roar so deafening it shook Olympus to its roots. And with it came Typhon himself, more vengeful even than before...! What then, Goddess?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: We fought. I prevailed. Oh, blast... I was supposed to weave a yarn. Erm, he kept trying to crush me with his chin... but I evaded, and clambered to the top of his head, where I drove the Sister Blades Lim and Oros into his brain!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, quite gruesome at the end there, I like it! My only criticism, if I may, is that... all too many tales involving huge monstrosities resolve that way. Erm, what if... he were to swallow you whole? Only for you to cut yourself out of his gullet?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Your goal is within reach, Goddess, that much I know just from how tense the good witch seems to be of late... not to pry but are you clear about what more needs to be done?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I believe so. I'd be inclined to tell you all of it, except you know how Headmistress gets about these things. Suffice it to say that the final phase of our plan is in progress.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: That's all I need to hear. This phase is up to you and her. For my part, it seems you're able to get to where you need to be consistently enough.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Found yourself a Phantom Lair in the Fields, if my Shades down there are still to be believed. Blood-sucking bastards... the Phantoms, not the Shades.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Don't such creatures usually stalk the surface in their search of living prey? They must go rather hungry in the Underworld.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Better that than burn under the break of day, or so they figure, probably. Well, you know what they're after next you meet again.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Found yourself a Phantom Lair in the Fields, if my Shades down there are still to be believed. Blood-sucking bastards... the Phantoms, not the Shades.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Don't such creatures usually stalk the surface in their search of living prey? They must go rather hungry in the Underworld.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Better that than burn under the break of day, or so they figure, probably. Well, you know what they're after next you meet again.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Goddess, latest reports said you encountered the King Vermin! Don't care if you're immortal, I'm relieved you made it back in one piece...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: It can't be. I did confront a rodent of extraordinary savagery, but it was so little! I assumed the King Vermin was huge.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Tall tales. The King Vermin's wrath is inversely proportional to its stature. And its stature, as I understand, is very small indeed...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Goddess, latest reports said you encountered the King Vermin! Don't care if you're immortal, I'm relieved you made it back in one piece...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: It can't be. I did confront a rodent of extraordinary savagery, but it was so little! I assumed the King Vermin was huge.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Tall tales. The King Vermin's wrath is inversely proportional to its stature. And its stature, as I understand, is very small indeed...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Heard reports you were proceeding well through Oceanus, but evidently they were not up-to-date. What brings you back, Goddess?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: A Sea-Serpent, if you must know. A particularly substantial one caught me out. You ever face them in your mortal voyages?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: I did, aye. But not alone! Even with my shipmates, seasoned veterans all, we scarce could track the slippery bastards darting out of the waters. Erm, not reassuring, is this?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Some of the drowned sailors whom you face up there whilst sailing through Poseidon's Rift were probably once men I used to know... not that I could recognize them now.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: There's one at least that seems to have a level of command over the rest, if fancier attire and weaponry are any indication. I fought him just last night on his big ship.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Likely the Yargonaut, if the reports add up. There was a band of similarly-named adventurers he may have hailed from. But they were from a bit before my time. Perhaps still trying to re-live their glory days...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Some of the drowned sailors whom you face up there whilst sailing through Poseidon's Rift were probably once men I used to know... not that I could recognize them now.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
