dialogueLines.text: There's one at least that seems to have a level of command over the rest, if fancier attire and weaponry are any indication. I fought him just last night on his big ship.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Likely the Yargonaut, if the reports add up. There was a band of similarly-named adventurers he may have hailed from. But they were from a bit before my time. Perhaps still trying to re-live their glory days...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Wanted to say, I appreciate your counsel, Odysseus. You've been like a brother to me. Or an uncle? One of those. You'd know better than I.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Look, you don't have to tell me what it's like not having a family about. I spent a good-sized portion of my mortal days away from home. Had a war to fight, got a little sidetracked after.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Sidetracked? Headmistress says that you spent many years at sea. Some of them in the company of goddesses, at that.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Aye, well. By the time I finally returned, I scarce recognized my son, and my wife... we lived out our mortal days together, and that was enough. I reckon they're comfortable somewhere below.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You haven't seen them? What happened?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Penelope and I drifted apart. Happens with mortals, sometimes. And it happens with Shades too. As for my son Telemachus, he's his own man. Such is life. Or death, as it turns out.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Come join us, Goddess! I was just regaling the Shades about when first I traveled to the Underworld. Whilst I yet lived and breathed, if you can believe it! And thusly I got out.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Come on, Odysseus, there was no escape from here when my father ruled. Are these Shades truly so impressionable?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh I'm dead serious! My tales aren't... quite as tall as they may seem! But now, the gates of hell are flung wide open, aren't they...?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Come join us, Goddess! I was just regaling the Shades about when first I traveled to the Underworld. Whilst I yet lived and breathed, if you can believe it! And thusly I got out.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Come on, Odysseus. Are these Shades truly so impressionable?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh I'm dead serious! My tales aren't... quite as tall as they may seem! But now, the gates of hell are flung wide open, aren't they...?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I was just thinking nothing clears the mind like a refreshing, burning-hot soak. Care to accompany me?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, I do enjoy a good relaxing bath, even whilst dead, and who am I to say no to a goddess... so why not!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: I know your customs and mine aren't quite the same, but my eyes are going to remain averted here, if that's all right.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Would mortals often bathe, Odysseus?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, erm, well... perhaps once in a while, when they could. Though, seldomly both men and women at the same time. Doing so could seem... untoward.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I see. We gods have no such shame, I realize. Have I made you uncomfortable?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Me? No, no, of course not! In fact I've bathed with goddesses before, it's just... it's been a little while.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Oh that's all right, doubtless you'll recall the finer points. You mostly just have to try and let go your burdens.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Should be simple, as I've many of those! In fact no matter how many I let go, it seems some yet remain. Though being here does help.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Well... as long as it's agreeable for you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Well! Don't know about you, but I am properly relaxed, I'd say!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Hey I was thinking I'm due for a good boil in the springs. Join me for a bit?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: A visit to the baths, with you, right now? Hah! Sure! I'm honored to accompany you, Goddess.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: I trust the temperature is to your liking, aye?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You've met many goddesses even in your mortal days, haven't you...?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: In this I'm very fortunate, indeed. The lady Athena often watched over me. I'd never have survived the war if not for her. Then, on my travels home, there was Calypso... and Circe...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Circe used to study under Headmistress, always with her crystals. And Calypso... she's a Nymph, correct? A lesser goddess I suppose.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Nothing lesser about her, with respect. These goddesses, they... enthralled me each in their own right. Almost made me forget myself. Forget my family.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: But you did not forget. You made your journey home. Even I know the tale.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: It's a tale, Goddess. Anyway, life takes its complicated turns. Take it from me.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: That was a splendid and much-needed time. Truly appreciate the invitation.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I've attempted to relax more lately and wanted to ask if you'd join me at the springs, in case you've a similar objective.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Well, if we're talking objectives now, then I most certainly cannot decline. And things have been quieter. After you.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: What am I to do with myself now, Odysseus...? My task is complete. I suppose I can keep doing it forever... this new phase of it we're in.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Is that what you wish to do, Goddess? I think you've earned the right to follow your heart for a bit.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I don't know what I wish to do... just keep going without stopping, I suppose. But slowing down like this is fine.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: I was never one to sit still either, though look at me now. It's difficult to know when a new sense of purpose strikes. I found mine most recently when I met the good witch. You may be in search of something new now. You'll find it.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: How can you be so certain? If I stay on this same path, I'll just keep visiting the same parts of the Underworld and the surface again and again.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: But you'll discover more along the way, of that I have no doubt at all. You're an immortal goddess; there is no need for you to rush.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Lord Hermes would never forgive me for saying it but... I just need to slow down, perhaps.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Ah, that was a pleasant time, though speaking with you generally is. Let's find the right pace for ourselves, aye?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Odysseus, how about alleviating the rigors of all this tactical planning with a visit to the springs?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Erm, I really do appreciate the offer, Goddess, just... I reckon your Headmistress would rather I stay here. You understand, don't you?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: You watch your back out there, all right?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Ready to take another stab at this, Goddess?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: If I were Chronos, I'd be properly afraid of you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Got everything here well under control.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Courage, Goddess. And no quarter to your enemies, all right?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Moonlight guide you, as you witches say.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Quite a mess we've gotten ourselves into, haven't we?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: I know things must seem dire, but take heart. We'll navigate through this.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Any luck finding the head of Chronos yet?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Be sure to hit them where it hurts for me, will you?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: All's well enough here. We'll see you when you're back.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Death to Chronos and all that, aye?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Beg pardon, Goddess, I must get this correspondence to the surface right away.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Fresh shipment of supplies came in whilst you were out.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Don't mind me, Goddess, I'm just busy drafting up some of our latest schemes.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: We've many sympathizers on the surface and below still loyal to your father.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Go forth and slaughter me some wretched traitors, will you, Goddess?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Once more you grace me with your presence here, Goddess.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Eh, would that we could talk like this a while, but I've a matter to resolve.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: The Nightmare Goddess stands before me in all her glory...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: You need anything at all, you know where to find me, Goddess.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: You come back safely, else I'll have to venture out there and haul you in myself.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: You'll have your vengeance, Goddess. I am sure of it.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: We'll dig ourselves out of this one yet, you rest assured, Goddess.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: May your witchery and silver always serve you well.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: No telling what the Fates may have in store for us, aye?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Always a pleasure to be in your company, Goddess.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Through hard times such as these, our bonds grow strong.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: The good witch is very proud of you, you know that, aye?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Our Shades shall keep us well apprised of your progress.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: There's not much worse than being separated from one's family, aye...?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: We'll back you on this every step of the way, as many nights as it requires, Goddess.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: You know better than anyone that not every night will go our way. Here's to this next one.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: We're keeping a close eye on your grandfather just in case, but he's been all right.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: The restoration of the Underworld is going to take quite a while, won't it?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: The good witch must be the world's greatest instructor, but you were a remarkable student.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: We've got Shade informants watching all that goes on from Olympus to Tartarus.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: We heard you made it to the summit of Olympus, and got to the result you sought.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Reports say you got to the depths of Tartarus, and must have taken care of things down there.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: I have these moments where you remind me of the good witch more and more.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: This is quite a journey that we're on. As long as we keep going at a proper pace.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: My family's doing well, it seems. I had the opportunity to visit them again.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: It's good to see that you and Nemesis are getting on all right.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Surprised that Eris sticks around, but then, she seems to like you, doesn't she?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Appreciate you stopping by though you look eager to get out there again.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Think you're due for a visit to the Underworld for a change?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Not visited the surface in a little while if you're up for it.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Hey I happened on a couple of those Mystery Seeds if you would like!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: We got a gift of Gemstones from the Shades who joined us recently, so here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Tell me you killed the bastard? Drove your staff into his wretched skull, slid a dagger in his gut? Eh, not that he'd die from that; works on mortals, though!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Chronos the Titan of Time is vanquished, Odysseus. I have his head right here! At least... I wish I did.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Got these Twin Lures, Odysseus, and I can't exactly use them on my own. You know the rules!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Fishing! My greatest weakness, Goddess! The waters and I haven't always mixed, but perhaps it'll go better for me now. Besides, I'm overdue for a short leave. Let's go!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Used to be I fished out of necessity alone. The earth would oft be stingy with her bounties, whilst the sea would yield plenty to us mortals, if we knew where to look.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: What's been the biggest difference for you since those times? Not having to eat for sustenance? Not having to watch your mortal body slowly fall apart as you grew older?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Heh, well! You already know a couple of those points if for some reason we were tasked to make a list, but... the biggest difference, if I had to choose, is that... I finally am free.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You're free? But as a mortal, weren't you a king? A mighty warrior, leader of men... and a husband, and a father! Here you have a place of honor, too, except... we dwell in shadows. Our deeds are never to be told.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Well, we shall see about that last bit, but for the rest! Perhaps I got all of the wanderlust out of my system whilst I lived and breathed. Most of it, anyhow!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: I thoroughly enjoyed our outing, though I still thoroughly hate fish.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Hey what if we stop by the fishing pier again? It's been a while, and standing patiently is far less indulgent than sipping Ambrosia, right?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: You make a compelling argument, Goddess! And, I ought to confront my discomfort with the waters more. Head on over and I'll be there soon.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Something I should tell you, by the way. These times we spend together, which I thoroughly enjoy, I... wouldn't want the other Shades to get the wrong impression.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: And what impression would that be? Oh, you mean their mortal preconceptions. A man and a goddess together can only mean one thing. End up one way.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Well yes, but it's a preconception rooted in events that have occurred... including to me. And even though our Shades are loyal, they do like to gossip, we know that.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I suppose. If anything they ought be gossiping about Headmistress and yourself, rather than us.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: What? Oh come on, Goddess, the good witch and I... we're like... oh blast, there must be some appropriate analogy...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I didn't mean to overreach, it's just... well, I can think of many reasons that Headmistress would inspire loyalty in someone such as you. And you in her.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Augh, you're as bad as those gossiping Shades, except I can't have you banished for your conduct. Now, what do you say we scoop some fish out of these waters, aye?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Ah. It's been good to chat away a bit instead of having to give counsel all the time, so cheers for that, Goddess. But, don't tell the good witch or anyone what you told me...?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Hey Odysseus? Got you something here I wanted you to have. For always keeping an eye out!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Goddess, your presence alone is all I could ask. Though, if you're in a giving mood, then perhaps you'll accept a small gift from me, in turn?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: I'm most grateful, Goddess, and expect to knock this back at the taverna at the soonest opportunity.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: No rush! Just as long as you enjoy it when that opportunity presents itself!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Well, would you look at that! How could I possibly say no? It seems you've found my weakness here, Goddess.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: The Great Tactician has no weaknesses, I'm sure! Though he does now have an extra bottle of Nectar.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: I ought to be the one honoring you, Goddess. My inclination is to save this for the moment of our victory, but it's good to have in reserve, in case of some emergency, you understand.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I understand, indeed! And you already honor me with your friendship, Odysseus... as well as with your knowledge about how to deal swift death!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: I ought to be the one honoring you, Goddess. My inclination is to save this, but it's good to have in reserve, in case of some emergency, you understand.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I understand, indeed! And you already honor me with your friendship, Odysseus... as well as with your knowledge about how to deal swift death!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh I see what you're up to, here, Goddess! If you continue to exploit my weakness to this stuff, I may yet learn to turn it to a strength. Good plan!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: What can I say? I learned from the best. May you grow stronger and stronger.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: You know, I journeyed far and wide throughout my mortal days, meeting great mortals and even greater gods, but... never could I have imagined ending up here, only to receive such a handsome gift from one such as you.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: They say life takes strange turns, and so apparently does death, Odysseus. Whatever the Fates may have in store, I hope you'll always be nearby to lend your counsel.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Well I do like to travel, but... I think you'll always know where to find me. Cheers for the Nectar... should last me through whatever hard times are to come.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Hey how about a visit to the taverna if you're not overly preoccupied right now? This Ambrosia here needs opening!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: That it most certainly does, but, and this pains me to say, I've too many reports right now requiring verification. A lot's been going on. So... perhaps later?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: How about you hold onto the bottle then? Later can be whenever you desire. Your Shade informants could even come along!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Hah, there's a thought! I'd likely never hear from them again. Though that would also mean I could visit the taverna more often...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: What would you say to a productive evening of inspiring more loyalty over at the taverna again? I enjoy our conversations there.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: I would say let's be off immediately, but I must say, I'm anticipating word from an informant soon and so need to stay put... I also mean... to visit the taverna somewhat less.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Oh! I apologize. It's not been my intent to keep attempting to draw you away from your responsibilities.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: It's nothing that you did, it's just... I knew the sorts who spent their days and nights feasting, taking leave of their senses. I swore never to be so indulgent. To always leave some of my desires unfulfilled.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I understand the feeling. To yearn for something is a driving force. Though, how about we give this bottle to your informant? For a duty well-performed.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: An excellent tactical choice. I'll tell them it was courtesy of the Princess of the Underworld herself!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Beautiful night, isn't it? The kind where one may catch brief glimpses of the Moon amid her race across the sky. She even left a little something for you here.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Moon Dust. You've no idea how much I could use this in my meditations recently...! Although you always have an idea, don't you?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Our Shades report good things about your Animal Familiars, you know. Not all such creatures get on well with spirits of the dead, so... here's a little token of appreciation!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Witch's Delight, cooked to perfection. Headmistress must have made this batch herself... does she know you're giving this to me?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Got something more for your Animal Familiars, Goddess. Argos here, he would have loved these in our mortal days, I reckon! Don't ask where I got them, just take them.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: It's requiring all of my resolve to heed your instructions, Odysseus, because Witch's Delight is not easy to come by. On behalf of my Familiars, thank you!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Your father used to safeguard entire stores of Gemstones but lately they're harder to come by. Now that our Broker's looking for them, I wanted you to have these.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: But aren't they yours? Wait, you're going to say they belong to the realm, so I might as well take them.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: The Shades you recently recruited down in Tartarus have organized a little gift, but... seemed too nervous to deliver it themselves. Here! Gemstones from the lowest depths.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: This is very generous of them! Please give them my thanks, and tell them they don't need to be afraid, now that they're here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: I have an offering for you, Goddess! On behalf of all the Shades residing here, we are honored to be in your service and stand ready to do whatever it takes to prevail! Please share these spoils with whomever you see fit.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Some Nectar, thank you! I feel like one of the gods of Olympus right now. You truly won't mind if I pass these bottles along?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: This one's on behalf of the Shades you recently invited to the Crossroads, Goddess. You've invigorated our ranks with these new recruits, who seem generous! Here.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Nectar bottles, thank you... all of you! I wonder where they found these, though can think of several acquaintances who would likely enjoy them.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: You want to practice with that Rod of Fishing, now at least we've got a solid pier for it. Here, I have a couple Lures to spare in case you ever want.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Thank you, I do like visiting the fishing pier! Both for the practice with the rod, and for a quiet place to chat.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: It's good to see the springs back there restored. When next you wish to use them, here... should keep the waters perfectly pristine.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Some Bath Salts, cheers for that. I know a lot's been happening, but we should all visit the springs sometime.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: You'll soon surpass the good witch at your current rate, Goddess! So say some of our Shades at least, and they make a persuasive argument. Here! A symbol of their faith.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: They would relinquish some of their Psyche, to benefit a task I never could achieve alone...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: You'll soon surpass the good witch at your current rate, Goddess! So say some of our Shades at least, and they make a persuasive argument. Here! A symbol of their faith.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: They would relinquish some of their Psyche, to benefit a task I never could achieve alone...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: It isn't much but our Shades out there, they... scraped together some materials that we reckon you could use. Here! Least we can do.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Ash of the Dead... Odysseus, are these...? Our Shades are giving up their own mortal remains. All for our cause.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: A bit of something for you here, Goddess. The gift of skeletal remains would not be looked on favorably back where I come from, but then... you've more respect for the dead than mortals generally did.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: More Bones than I can quickly count, or locate on my own. Then I shall leave them in the Wretched Broker's care!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: I'm not the sort to dwell too much about what the Three Fates may have in store for us, Goddess, but... I do have a bit of Fabric we attribute to their work. And now it's yours.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Fate Fabric! Few better ways to ensure the cauldron properly conducts a magickal effect. I could definitely use this if you'd be so kind.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: I'm not the sort to dwell too much about what the Three Fates may have in store for us, Goddess, but... I do have a bit of Fabric we attribute to their work. And now it's yours.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Fate Fabric! Few better ways to ensure the cauldron properly conducts a magickal effect. I could definitely use this if you'd be so kind.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Back in my mortal days, bronze was the most precious metal that I knew, so quickly could it cut a man's life short. But I've come to understand your appreciation for silver! Go on. Take it! Our Shades and I, we practically insist.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Truly? Silver so pure isn't easy to come by, even with the proper tools. I'll make good use of it, for all our sakes.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: We managed to recover some materials from the surface and thought you might make good use of them, Goddess. Whoever this Bronze used to belong to, they're long dead.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: It's a versatile metal, thank you. The Nocturnal Arms I wield seem to hunger for it, as well as silver and more.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: You're on the prowl for ingredients that can go into that pot, aren't you, Goddess? Here, perhaps you can use these. No mere flowers, I've been told.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Fresh Molies...! The roots are still perfectly black. Several incantations I've been mulling all would benefit from these. How did you know?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: You're on the prowl for ingredients that can go into that pot, aren't you, Goddess? Here, perhaps you can use these. No mere flowers, I've been told.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Fresh Molies...! The roots are still perfectly black. Several incantations I've been mulling all would benefit from these. How did you know?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Saw you've been working with Hypnos over there. Once when I was attempting to suppress his snoring I found these tucked in the folds of his... is it a blanket or a cloak...?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: These are seeds for sleep-inducing Poppy flowers! I may well need these if he's ever going to wake up. Thank you! And, I think it's a cloak?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Got something here for you, Goddess. For whenever our little garden over there could use a little extra greenery... or if you're ever left unsure about what to plant next.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Thank you, Odysseus! But, what are these? I try to keep whatever seeds I find well-organized.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: I've never seen a little garden thrive like ours there. Near-effortless results! Then I remember who your mother is and understand. Here, to keep it flourishing.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Thank you! I suppose my mother being the goddess of verdure may be why I have a knack for growing plants. Or my grandmother being goddess of seasons.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Braggart. You ought to take up farming if the witchery doesn't work out!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Been holding onto something for a while that I wanted you to have. This bottle of Ambrosia I once got from a goddess, so... it's only fitting that I give it to another. I think it'll pick up a much better flavor with you. Here, I practically insist.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Odysseus, are you certain I can have this? Perhaps we all can share it at the taverna on some fitting occasion.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Odysseus, have you seen Hecate about?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Mm, not in a little while, little goddess. Though, I'd expect that pointy hat of hers to give her away!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Fair warning I'm a proper wretch right now, Goddess. Used to think I lived a complete life! But all your journeys made me realize that there was much I left unsaid. Too much.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: What? How do my journeys factor into that? And cease your wretchedness at once... or tell me how to make it stop.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Eh, you've met Scylla and the Sirens, the Cyclops Polyphemus, the Enchantress Circe... one way or another, they each left an indelible mark on me; to say nothing of my wife and son, wherever they are. I think... I need to go...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Go where? Odysseus, you can't leave now, we need you here! There must be something we can do.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Eugh. Probably there is, but I — I wouldn't wish to overburden you. You already take on far too much.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Apologies, I've... started letting my misery break free. Moments of weakness, becoming more frequent than I care to admit. I've been thinking about what to do... a plan.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: What is it? You mentioned Scylla, Polyphemus, Circe... I see them frequently enough that I could speak to them on your behalf if you'd like?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, I'd not ask you to do that. However, your connection to my Knuckle Bones is strong enough, perhaps I could relay a message to them myself. Just get my Keepsake there and I could do the rest?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: A spirit projection... I could probably maintain one for a little while. You mean to confront your old acquaintances?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: For now, that's all, Goddess. If aiding me this way won't stray from your objectives.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: I'm grateful that you let me pay a visit to some of my former associates, Goddess. It changed nothing, really, but... I felt as though I put several matters to rest. As for what happens now... I may be gone a little while...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Things are settled here enough. Take all the time you need, and you'll be welcome whenever you return. Can you say where you're going?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: ...To find my mortal family... my wife and son. I've done it before. May it not take quite as long on this occasion, though.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: It won't. You must have charted every passageway through the Underworld by now! But what will you do then?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: I'll figure that out on the way. In the meantime, I've deputized some of our Shades to keep an eye on things, and privately said my goodbyes to our good witch. Be well, in my absence.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: You be wary of the sorts you meet out there, all right? You find a live one gives you any trouble, you know where to hit him where it hurts.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I take it you heard about the Fates? Apparently everyone already has, figured you'd be among the first. Though come to think of it, I've not heard you speak about them much...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: ...It's because deep down I've always felt we make our choices for ourselves. Admittedly a controversial point of view when we've strong evidence that those three often choose for us, but... I have to heed my instincts on this one.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Wait, you don't believe in what they do? Their prophecies, the coming of a new age... none of it?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: I wouldn't go that far, but... I choose to believe in those I know and trust, including members of your family. If that makes me a fool, then so be it.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: So be it? Sounds like accepting fate to me.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: This new age the Fates foretold, when they'll hand it over to mortals, by which I think they meant the big decisions... what say our Shades about all that?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: I've heard a mix of viewpoints, though they've been enthusiastic on the whole. Ungrateful wretches, aye? We're only here by the grace of the gods.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: It isn't always grace, I suppose. Many of these Shades met an early demise due to acts of gods. They don't want to be pushed around.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: There shall always be someone or something to push such people around. Whether it's a god or not hardly matters. The gods can have their moods, I know. But I'd not have gone far without them. It's why I'm here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: You found a path to victory and got us there. Wouldn't have been much of a plan if it didn't leave room to improvise! Of course our plan now also has this other phase as a result...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Cleaning up after Chronos... we all thought I would get rid of him. But, I found another way... one I think shall leave my family better off, if not the rest of the world, eventually.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: I well and truly understand, Goddess, and do believe your choice was wise. As for what's next, everything is ready for you to proceed if and when you are.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Eliminating real possibilities that we could have failed... ensuring time flows freely forth, as Chronos puts it. I don't quite know what to expect, but the change we're fighting for won't happen overnight. What do you make of it?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Eh, my personal opinion is it doesn't hurt to try! To the extent you'd other matters to resolve, consider it an opportunity to do so. And, we'll always be anticipating your return.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Everything proceeding as expected in the new phase of your task thus far, Goddess? Stretching the definition of expected, perhaps, as this is... new territory for us all.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: It's an unusual assignment, ensuring time flows freely. If done correctly, everything will seem as before. And even if not... there's no guarantee anything will go wrong.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Well that makes this sound more familiar than I thought. Life in all its uncertainties! Anyway, we're watching Chronos and ensuring that your route remains intact.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Sounds like your father's House is back in some semblance of order. Always wanted to visit if ever I could, if only to greet an old comrade or two from the war.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I'd also like to visit again, but there's no telling when if ever we would have that chance so long as we're Unseen. Officially they're not even supposed to know we're here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: I know, but it doesn't make the desire go away! At any rate, as long as the servants of the King and Queen are well. Perhaps our paths shall later cross, who knows.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Is Typhon really up there on the summit still? Hm, no trace of him in our reports, but if Chronos... erm, your grandfather, that is... if he's using some sort of chronomancy to transport you...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: He is, yes. And I end up confronting Typhon as before. Apparently these are possibilities within the flow of time in which I haven't prevailed... until I do.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: I don't see how that's possible. What, there are all these other instances of you out there, some failing, some prevailing, all at the same time? Nonsense... but then again, who am I to question bizarre plans?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: If you're not too caught up in your reports right now, what do you say we put this bottle of Ambrosia to its proper use?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: I am caught up in my reports, Goddess! Focused on what matters most... meet you at the taverna.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: ...We mortals had what we called wine, an acrid sort of drink made of crushed grapes. We'd sometimes say it tasted as good as Ambrosia, but... it most certainly did not.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You speak of so many hardships from your mortal days and nights and yet, there's always this sense of longing there... you miss those times, don't you?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Such moments and occasions do occur. I know a lot more now than I did then, and... sometimes I miss that feeling... knowing less. Or the camaraderie of sailing with my crew.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: But many of your crew are with you here now, right? Even the Shade of your old dog. How can you miss them if they're still around?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: We're Shades, Goddess, and... not all quite as whole as I. Besides, you still can miss your past with those you still know now! Remember when you used to play at hiding with our good witch, back when you were very small?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I never thought that we were playing; she was teaching me to hunt for unseen prey. But, I do see your point.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Well my original point was merely that this Ambrosia's really something else! Let us ensure it does not go to waste.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: I'd not realized how much I needed that. It's true what they say, that Ambrosia tastes much better in good company.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: How go the reports this evening, or rather, do you have occasion for a taverna break perhaps?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Reckon I could join you for just such an occasion, now that you raise the possibility, Goddess. You ready now?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: ...I fought alongside quite a number of these Shades, you know. Loyal in life as well as now in death... where do you suppose that comes from? Loyalty, I mean.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Having a sense of purpose or a cause. And, a basic desire for stability and truth. Loyalty is consistency.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Aye to all that, but it is even simpler to me! Loyalty comes from individuals. It's like a form of love; some give it, some receive it, sometimes it's mutual, sometimes it's not.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Headmistress always taught me never to demand loyalty. Loyalty wears thin if not for trust, she'd say. Did your fellow Shades follow you out of loyalty or obligation?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Hm! A mix of each. The good witch is right that the best kind of loyalty is given. I reckon you inspire that sort, Goddess; the both of you. I'd not have stayed here this long otherwise.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Chronos certainly could have used someone of your skill and experience. With all his promises of a new Golden Age, surely he commands quite a bit of loyalty himself.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: I tried serving tyrants before; wasn't for me. Now, what do you say we inspire some more loyalty right now? Let's have these Shades come join us for a round.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: How is it that we got so much done on what was meant to be a break? Anyhow, the Shades were grateful! As am I.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Even I get a break from time to time, as should you!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Just having a quick drink here with our fellow Shades.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Turns out I can take reports in the taverna here, Goddess!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Lively bunch we've got for a bunch of deadbeats, aye?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: You see that, lads? I told you she'd turn up!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Stick around a bit, Goddess! You've earned some rest.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: The Tactician brings ill tidings with an optimistic bent. Fine. And what news from the men of the isles, if any yet survive?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: According to my confidants, they've all sworn in with Chronos, I'm afraid. Mortals... they often want whatever they don't have. Another Golden Age, in this case. The promise of one.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: 'Tis a lie that spurs their betrayal, rather than a promise. They shall perish, then... by the wrath of the gods.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: And then their wretched souls shall hound us with renewed vengefulness. I've witnessed endless wars before, Madam. So have you gods. How can we stop this?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: We cannot. Melinoë, however? Certainly you can, correct?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: ...And, erm, that's the end of my report this time, Madam. Should you have any questions, please don't hesitate to summon me at any time.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: I shan't, Odysseus. Although, there is one further matter that I wanted to discuss with you, and rather quietly...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: ...In short, the reconstruction is proceeding, though not at a brisk pace, and the damage on the surface is contained. Wounds that shall slowly heal. We'll see what scars remain.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hecate_01
dialogueLines.text: Sister Selene makes a similar assessment. Well, then... what shall you do from here, Odysseus? You could visit the surface whilst the Underworld remains in disrepair.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh I may wander for a bit, Madam, but... by your leave, I'd like to keep the Crossroads as my base of operations for the time. I reckon we've a good thing going here, don't we?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: ...You have my full report, Madam. Do let me know of any questions or objections.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Do let me know if any part of my performance has been lacking as of late, Madam.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: ...No need to trouble yourself over that, Madam. We've everything under control.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: ...I've noted everything we discussed for future reference, Madam. Until our next review.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: ...If there's anything more that I could do for you, Madam, do not hesitate to ask.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: ...I'm forever at your service, Madam. Or you banish me or I decide to wander off.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: Master Odysseus and I were trading tales of deadly peril once again.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: And we have many such tales to trade, as it turns out!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: I can never get enough of the tales of your voyages, Master Odysseus.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Nor can I ever get enough of telling them exactly as they occurred!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: No mortal had more near-death experiences than Master Odysseus here!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: How ironic that I caught up with Master Odysseus here of all places.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Well, better here than anywhere else I can think of!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: Princess, may you be as crafty and resilient as Master Odysseus here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Moros_01
dialogueLines.text: Such a wonder one could leave their mortal coil with so many tales to tell!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: What can I say, it was an exciting time!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: ...I'm saying that the possibility exists. Eternal vigilance is near impossible. Enemies have always exploited the lack thereof... I ever tell you of the old wooden horse trick?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nemesis_01
dialogueLines.text: The old wooden horse trick, yeah. Surely your reputation as the Great Tactician hinges on more than this one tale?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Odysseus_01
dialogueLines.text: Ah, but classics ought to be retold! Though, if you'd rather, I have far more tales of trickery and valor. Take the Cave of the Cyclops!
